summary:
In this case, the child presents symptoms indicative of an infectious process affecting meninges; specifically, raised WBCs predominantly PMNs & hypoglycorrhachia point towards probable bacterial etiology like Pyogenic Meningitis rather than other causes such as TB or HSV given normoimaging studies.
The differential diagnoses have been narrowed down based primarily upon abnormal cerebrospinal fluid analysis results which are characteristic for bacterial infections over others mentioned considering negative neuro-imaging study outcomes here too. To confirm further testing including culture sensitivity should follow up next steps clinically managing suspected condition appropriately per guidelines available currently regarding empirical
